headline: Quebec Firm Develops Cleaner Gold Mining Method
meta_description: A Quebec chemical company has created a cyanide-free method to extract gold, significantly reducing environmental harm from mining operations.
summary_short: Dundee Sustainable Technologies has pioneered a non-toxic alternative to traditional gold extraction, reducing mining's environmental footprint.
body:
Mining has long relied on chemicals like cyanide to extract gold from ore, creating serious environmental risks. Now a Quebec-based firm, Dundee Sustainable Technologies, has developed a cyanide-free method that maintains extraction efficiency while eliminating toxic exposure.

The company has also created GlassLock, a patented process that tackles another major mining challenge: handling arsenic and other hazardous byproducts safely. These innovations allow mining operations to meet environmental standards more easily and reduce their toxic burden on surrounding ecosystems.

The breakthrough matters because it shows that industries built on harmful practices can evolve. By offering companies a genuinely safer alternative, this technology creates room for mining to continue while protecting both workers and the environment from unnecessary poison.
